findings.locus: §2.4 GateDecision — origin_mechanism as live property with authored reserved; §2.6 / §5 CandidatePromotion; §5 retraction shape
findings.claim: DDR-002's stated scope (Decision.6, Rationale) is that it 'owns the schema contract only' and routes architecture, feedback-loop governance, and service realization elsewhere. But §2.4 decides a forward-compatibility posture ('authored reserved-and-available for a future SOFIA-as-authoritative-decider state, ADR-001 Position 4, expressible without re-architecture'), which is a design-lifecycle/trajectory commitment, not a node/relationship/constraint/index fact. This commitment is never surfaced as a numbered sub-decision and rides along inside a node schema.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 Decision.6 ('This DDR owns the schema contract only') vs DDR-002 §2.4 GateDecision forward-compatibility posture

findings.locus: Change Log v1.1.0 (2026-06-22, RBT-43) vs Decision block
findings.claim: The Change Log records that checks #20 and #21, the ProvenanceSummary node, RETRACTS edge, and archived status were added as an additive MINOR amendment (CI-only set 19 → 21). These are load-bearing schema decisions, but the top-level Decision block (Decision.1–.6) never enumerates retraction, the ProvenanceSummary/provenance-survival guarantee, or the archived terminal state as decided components — they appear only in body prose. The title/summary/decision-content match is incomplete: substantive decisions in the body are not reflected in what the record claims to decide.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 Decision.1–.6 vs §5 (retraction shape / ProvenanceSummary) + §2.3 (archived) + Change Log RBT-43

findings.authority_locus: author-decision-record-SKILL — DDR-002 Decision section discipline ('Then expand the ruling's components as numbered subsections if needed'; 'State decisions testably') + ddr-template Decision section

findings.claim: ADR-002 §2.2 states 'Community was trialed for it and failed' and asserts 'The rejection is empirically established and ratified,' then locates the durable evidence in 'DDR-001's spike-findings section.' But DDR-001's Spike Findings section explicitly records that 'the specific edition-feature blocker from the original trial is not in the captured record and is not reconstructed here.' The ADR's prose claims an empirically-established rejection whose defining evidence (the specific capability blocker) the cited home admits it does not hold — the decision leans on support the substrate does not supply.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-002 §2.2 ('empirically established and ratified' + edition capability the plane model depends on) vs DDR-001 Spike Findings ('specific edition-feature blocker ... not reconstructed here')

findings.claim: §6 states 'Compliance is currently aspirational' and 'Mechanized enforcement ... is not built yet.' The ADR template §6 requires that an unbuilt compliance mechanism 'point at a tracking item.' No tracking pointer accompanies the aspirational-compliance admission; the deferred consequence is declared but its tracking artifact is not named.

cited_authority.ref: adr-template §6 ('Compliance is currently aspirational, tracked at {{ticket}}')

findings.authority_locus: adr-template §6 ('Compliance is currently aspirational, tracked at {{ticket}}, ... point at a tracking item')

findings.locus: Feedback-Loop Architecture — third component-scoped author class
findings.claim: DDR-001 introduces a third write-authority author class (the scheduled feedback-loop job authoring CandidatePromotion, plus gateway-authored provenance snapshot and materialized KG node) and states ADR-002 §2.6 'neither closes against nor extends to the feedback loop's own writes.' This is a new write-authority decision homed under ADR-002's principle but not enumerated by ADR-002 §2.6. It is surfaced (Decision.5, conformance check 7), so scope is declared — but the claim that ADR-002 §2.6 'neither closes against nor extends to' the feedback-loop writes is a reading of ADR-002 that ADR-002 itself does not state; DDR-001 is asserting the silence of an upstream authority as license to extend it.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-001 Feedback-Loop Architecture ('ADR-002 §2.6 neither closes against nor extends to') vs ADR-002 §2.6 (fixes ASA/AOE assignment; makes no statement about feedback-loop writes)

findings.claim: DDR-001 repeatedly routes governance concerns to DDR-003 (feedback-loop governance) as a sibling and forward dependency, and ADR-001 §2.5/§7 name DDR-003 as 'forthcoming.' DDR-003 is not in the document set under review and its status (PROPOSED/ACCEPTED/nonexistent) is undeclared. DDR-001's dependence on an artifact whose existence and status are unstated leaves a named dependency unresolved at this altitude — DDR-001 declares the dependency but not whether the depended-on artifact exists.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-001 Cross-References ('DDR-003 — feedback-loop governance') + ADR-001 §7 ('DDR-003, forthcoming') vs absent DDR-003 with undeclared status

findings.locus: §1 / §2 / §5 — scope routing to DDR-001 / DDR-003 / SDDs
findings.claim: I checked whether DDR-002's decision content stays within its claimed schema-contract scope at each substantive boundary (feedback-loop mechanics, governance policy, gateway API, retrieval affordance, retention policy). At the boundaries that route mechanics/policy elsewhere (§5 ownership line, Boundary Routing Map, Named Gaps), the record consistently names what it owns (node/edge/constraint shape) versus what it cites, and the routing targets are explicitly identified. The scope discipline holds across those routings.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 Decision.6 + §5 ownership line + Cross-References Boundary Routing Map

findings.authority_locus: DDR-002 Decision.6 + §5 ownership line + Cross-References Boundary Routing Map (the record's own ratified scope-routing content)

findings.claim: I tried to fault the no-CMEK decision as an undeclared scope commitment. The record cleanly scopes it to the initial build, names the enabling constraint (no-PHI-by-design, classification-enforced), declares the consequence (§5.2 constraint, §5.3 risk with mitigation), and defers a PHI scope-change to its own ADR. The decision decides exactly what it claims, with its dependency (classification enforcement) and side effect (compliance obligation) surfaced. Held.

cited_authority.ref: sofia-vision operating-environment constraints ('no PHI by design ... no CMEK in the initial build; a future PHI scope change is its own ADR')

findings.authority_locus: sofia-vision operating-environment constraints ('no PHI by design ... no CMEK in the initial build; a future PHI scope change is its own ADR')

findings.claim: I checked whether ADR-001 claims to decide the artifact vocabulary/schema (which would overreach into DDR-002's scope). §2.2 explicitly marks ReasoningProgress/Evidence/RejectedAlternative as illustrative and defers canonical labels, properties, and vocabulary to DDR-002; §2.5 commits only the principle that the promotion mechanism exists and is EA-gated, deferring governance to DDR-003. The record decides the conceptual capture contract and no more. Claim-fidelity held.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-001 §2.2 ('illustrative ... owned by the Graph Schema document (DDR-002)') + §2.5 deferral to DDR-003

findings.authority_locus: ADR-001 §2.2 (labels/vocabulary deferred to DDR-002) + §2.5 (governance deferred to DDR-003); author-decision-record-SKILL altitude-routing (ADR = platform principle, schema/vocabulary → DDR)

findings.locus: §2.4 Governance — GateDecision.DECIDED_ON vs §3 edge catalog / §5 bridge edges
findings.claim: DDR-002 asserts (§2.4) that the GateDecision verdict rides `DECIDED_ON {outcome}` → Solution, and §5 declares `(GateDecision)-[:DECIDED_ON {outcome}]->(Solution)`. But §3's Decision/audit edge catalog lists `(GateDecision)-[:REVIEWED {hash}]->(Artifact)` and does not list any `DECIDED_ON` edge, while `DECIDED_ON` appears only under the §5 promotion/solution edges. The GateDecision→Solution `DECIDED_ON` edge is used by the selection mechanism (§5 'Selection among produced candidates') and by conformance check #15's contrast, yet the core edge catalog (§3) that purports to enumerate the Governance sub-graph edges omits it — an internal inconsistency about where the load-bearing gate verdict edge is defined.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §3 edge catalog omits GateDecision-[:DECIDED_ON]->Solution while §2.4/§5 depend on it

findings.claim: §5 states that a successor to a `conditional` node must not be consumed `unconditional` absent an explicit EA re-scoping, and explicitly says this is 'a failure §7 check #19 cannot catch because the successor would no longer be marked conditional.' The document then commits only an 'integrity expectation' and routes the carry-forward mechanism to the knowledge-service SDD, leaving no enforceable invariant in the schema layer for a named safety-relevant over-admission failure (consuming EA-conditioned knowledge out of scope). The claim that conditional knowledge 'is never consumed out-of-scope' (§7 #19, 'blocking safety control') is therefore not substantiated for the supersession case the document itself surfaces.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §5 acknowledged gap in #19 coverage on supersession vs. its stated blocking safety guarantee

findings.claim: §7's provenance existence constraint is scoped to 'KG nodes' plus enumerated RG/Artifact types. GateDecision is a Governance-plane KG node whose §2.4 schema treats `origin_mechanism` as a 'live property' currently `ingested`. This is consistent, but §2.4 also states the intermediate case (SOFIA issues, external holds authoritative record) 'has no current instance'; the schema provides no discriminator today between `ingested` (external-captured) and a future `authored` SOFIA-authoritative gate other than the source_record_ref presence rule (#17). The reader is left to infer that #17 alone distinguishes the two live values — the constraint is stated but the distinguishing invariant is not cross-linked at §2.4.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §2.4 GateDecision origin_mechanism live-values + DDR-002 §7 #17 source_record_ref rule

findings.locus: ADR-002 §2.2 / §4.1 Community-trial substantiation vs DDR-001 Spike Findings
findings.claim: ADR-002 §2.2 and §4.1 assert the Community-edition rejection is 'empirically established and ratified' and route its durable home to 'DDR-001's spike-findings section.' DDR-001's Spike Findings section explicitly records that 'the specific edition-feature blocker from the original trial is not in the captured record and is not reconstructed here.' The claim substantiating Enterprise-as-requirement therefore rests on a spike whose determinative finding (which Enterprise capability Community lacked) is, by the cited authority's own admission, absent from the record. The substantiation ADR-002 §2.2 claims ('records the specific Enterprise capability the plane model depends on') is not met by the cited locus.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-002 §2.2/§4.1 substantiation claim vs DDR-001 Spike Findings recovery-boundary note

findings.locus: §7 check #17 vs §1 source_record_ref applicability on derived
findings.claim: §1 states `source_record_ref` is 'applicable as a source/stream ref on derived-from-external' and 'N/A on authored/promoted'. §7 #17 enforces it on `ingested` and on `derivation_class: distilled` (scoped explicitly to distilled, not aggregated). This is internally reconciled, but §1's phrasing 'derived-from-external' is not identical to §7's 'distilled', and a `derived`/`aggregated` node that happened to reference an external source would be permitted-but-unenforced. The §1↔§7 alignment is asserted but the vocabulary ('derived-from-external' vs 'distilled') is not fully unified.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §1 source_record_ref applicability wording vs §7 #17 scoping to distilled

findings.locus: ADR-001 §2.2 illustrative artifact kinds vs DDR-002 canonical RG vocabulary — Evidence provenance
findings.claim: ADR-001 §2.2's capture invariant states each reasoner's output 'is recorded with evidence linkage, source attribution by category, and an authoritative flag.' DDR-002 §4 makes `Evidence` the sole surrogate-only RG type carrying NO provenance group and NO authoritative flag, recovering provenance structurally via SOURCED_FROM. Whether a KG-sourced Evidence surrogate satisfies ADR-001's 'source attribution by category' obligation for that node is not reconciled: ADR-001's invariant is stated per captured-reasoning artifact, and DDR-002 exempts Evidence from carrying source attribution as a property. The conformance claim (DDR-002 realizes ADR-001's capture invariant) is not substantiated for the Evidence type against ADR-001 §2.2's per-artifact attribution requirement.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-001 §2.2 (source attribution by category, authoritative flag per captured artifact) vs DDR-002 §4 Evidence surrogate-only posture

findings.locus: §7 CI-only invariant set — enforcement posture vs ADR-002 §2.7 / DDR-002 §1 no-PHI
findings.claim: DDR-002 §7 #13 (no-PHI) is listed in the safety-critical tier as 'classification, gateway-enforced,' but the enforcement posture paragraph states the entire CI-only set is 'review-enforced (three-hat / authoring time), not CI-enforced' until mechanization lands. No-PHI is simultaneously described as gateway-enforced (§1: 'single no-PHI enforcement point') and as an aspirational CI-only check awaiting mechanization (§7). Whether PHI exclusion is actually enforced at initial build — on which ADR-002 §2.7's no-CMEK soundness explicitly depends — is left contradictory within DDR-002.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §1 (gateway-enforced no-PHI) vs §7 (no-PHI is CI-only, review-enforced until mechanization lands)

findings.claim: DDR-001 repeatedly routes governance concerns (retention policy, EA criteria, archival policy, thresholds) to DDR-003, and ADR-001 §2.5/§7 cite DDR-003 as 'forthcoming.' DDR-003 is not present in the document set under review, yet DDR-001 and DDR-002 are ACCEPTED with numerous load-bearing obligations (RG retention, archival trigger, multi-condition interim posture) deferred to a document that does not yet exist. The cross-reference resolves to a named-but-absent artifact; the deferral targets cannot be confirmed to exist.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-001/DDR-002 cross-references to DDR-003 (governance) — named artifact absent from the set

findings.claim: Checked the promoted-node provenance chain for conformance to ADR-001's auditability commitment and DDR-001 check #6 (promoted distinguishable from ingested): the chain PROMOTES_TO_KNOWLEDGE ← CandidatePromotion ← governing DECIDED_ON edge → PromotionDecision, keyed off the latest-decided_at governing edge, with origin_mechanism: promoted stamping, and the un-promotion mirror (#21 retraction-gated), holds internally — a superseded-verdict promotion and an unauthorized un-promotion are both detectable by the stated invariants. The surface is load-bearing and the invariant set is self-consistent as written.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-001 §3 (auditability structural) + DDR-001 conformance check #6, checked against DDR-002 §5/§7 #15/#21

The commitment propagates without contradiction and each downstream record cites the authority it conforms to; the surface held.

The extension does not silently contradict the upstream assignment; conformance holds.

findings.claim: Attacked the Position-5-on-a-trajectory-toward-Position-4 commitment for premature/over-commitment: whether committing a directional north-star (Position 4) before any service or per-phase design exists over-commits the platform to an unreachable end-state. It holds — the ADR explicitly frames Position 4 as a non-binary trajectory ('may never be strictly reached'), keeps the capture-discipline invariant as the only hard commitment, and defers per-stage mechanics as 'provisional' (§2.4), so the timing posture is proportionate to what is settled.

findings.claim: Attacked ADR-002 for reversibility-proportionality: it commits a hard-to-reverse edition + runtime choice (Neo4j Enterprise, self-managed GKE) while deferring production topology. Checked whether the hard commitment carries justification proportionate to undoing cost — it does: the edition rests on a ratified spike (Community failed the plane model), the GKE runtime is a forced exception (no managed Neo4j on GCP), and only the genuinely workload-dependent axis (topology) is deferred. Posture is sound.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-002 §2.2 substitution contract + §5.2 topology deferral

findings.authority_locus: ADR-002 §2.2 substitution contract + §5.2 topology deferral (the ratified spike grounding edition choice)

findings.locus: ADR-001 §2.5 / §5.3 — Position-4 trajectory engine deferred to unwritten DDR-003
findings.claim: The Position-4 trajectory — ADR-001's central non-rhetorical differentiator over a static Position 5 — is made real solely by the EA-gated promotion mechanism whose entire governance is deferred to DDR-003, which does not exist in the set and has no draft. ADR-001 §5.3 itself flags that if the mechanism 'is never built the trajectory becomes rhetorical.' The ADR is ACCEPTED and multiple downstream records (DDR-001, DDR-002) commit substantial schema (CandidatePromotion, PROMOTES_TO_KNOWLEDGE, ProvenanceSummary, retraction) against a governance authority that has never been ratified — a commitment to a position whose load-bearing dependency is unsettled.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-001 §2.5/§5.3 commits the trajectory engine; DDR-001 §Feedback-Loop and DDR-002 §5 build schema against DDR-003 governance that is absent from the set

findings.locus: §7 named exposure window — safety-critical CI-only invariants unmechanized across an accepted schema
findings.claim: DDR-002 is ACCEPTED and declared to gate twelve downstream SDDs, yet its entire safety-critical invariant tier (#1, #7, #13, #14, #15, #16, #17, #19, #21 — including no-PHI, write-authority, no-unapproved-promotion, no-unauthorized-retraction) is CI-only and unmechanized, enforced only by three-hat review. The set accepts a schema whose ground-truth-integrity guarantees depend entirely on a mechanization effort ('conformance-mechanization') that is neither scheduled nor ratified as a decision record. Accepting a system-of-record schema whose safety controls are aspirational is a posture commitment that deserves an explicit gating decision on when the gateway may be authored against it.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §7 'Named exposure window + sequencing' + ADR-002 §2.7 no-PHI enforcement dependency

findings.locus: DDR-002 §2.4 GateDecision.origin_mechanism 'authored' reserved for Position-4 SOFIA-as-decider
findings.claim: DDR-002 §2.4 reserves-and-makes-available an `origin_mechanism: authored` GateDecision path for 'a future SOFIA-as-authoritative-decider state (Position 4)' — schema that lets SOFIA itself author authoritative SDLC gate decisions. This anticipates a platform-posture shift (SOFIA making authoritative approval decisions rather than capturing externally-made ones) that ADR-001 treats as the audacious end-state never explicitly ratified as reachable, and that ADR-001 §2.5 subordinates to an EA-gated promotion mechanism. Baking a forward-compatible authoritative-decider surface into the schema commits the platform toward a position that arguably deserves its own decision record before the schema affordance is fixed.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-002 §2.4 GateDecision authored-reserved vs ADR-001 §2.1 Position 4 as unratified end-state / §2.2 human-override authority

findings.locus: no-vector-store reversibility framing across the three records
findings.claim: Attacked the no-vector-store commitment as a hard-to-reverse posture stated once and inherited downstream. Checked whether reversal cost is honestly acknowledged proportionate to blast radius — it is: ADR-002 §5.3 frames re-introduction as 'a store-set change with real blast radius, not a routine trigger' with an explicit ADR trigger, and DDR-002 §1 names the positive graph-native substitute rather than leaving the rejection bare. The reversibility posture is proportionate and coherent across records.

cited_authority.ref: ADR-002 §2.4/§4.3/§5.3 no-vector reversibility + DDR-002 §1 no-vector positive half

findings.authority_locus: ADR-002 §5.3 (re-introduction framed as store-set change with ADR trigger) + DDR-002 §1 (graph-native positive substitute)

findings.locus: DDR-003 referenced as sibling authority for policy across both DDRs while absent from the set
findings.claim: Both DDR-001 and DDR-002 route a large, load-bearing volume of policy (retention windows, EA approval criteria, archival triggers, remedy-boundary supersession-vs-retraction, multi-condition governance, judgment-consolidation health) forward to DDR-003, which is not present in the review set and has no ratified status. The architecture/governance split is asserted as 'settled,' but the governance half has no ratified home — the timing question of whether these DDRs should stand fully accepted while their designated policy authority is unwritten is worth recording.

cited_authority.ref: DDR-001 §Cross-References 'architecture/governance split is settled' + DDR-002 §Cross-References → DDR-003 forward-routing, DDR-003 absent from set

findings.claim: Checked the ASA-authors-ReasoningProgress / AOE-owns-ReasoningSession-lifecycle assignment across all four documents for drift: ADR-002 §2.6 fixes it, DDR-001 §Reasoning Graph and conformance check 3 restate it, and DDR-002 §4 write-authority and §7 check #7 realize it identically.
